conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm inquiring about my child. I have one child - Alex, who is 4 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 4 years old.

Now I need to ask: Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with Alex and are you responsible for their care (such as providing for their food, clothing, and general wellbeing)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex does live with me, but... their other parent also lives with us and they're the one who actually claims the Child Benefit already.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is an important detail.

Just to confirm: Alex's other parent is currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex, and you all live together in the same household. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. We all live together and Alex's other parent is already getting the Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that information.

Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any given time. Since Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you would not be eligible to claim it as well - even though you live together and share responsibility for Alex's care.

If circumstances change in the future (for example, if the other parent stops claiming or you separate), you could potentially claim Child Benefit at that time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time because Alex's other parent is already receiving Child Benefit for them. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to reach out again.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority; Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 4 years old and lives with the claimant, which would normally meet the age requirement (under 16) and care responsibility criteria. However, according to Child Benefit rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. Alex's other parent is already claiming and receiving Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to also claim Child Benefit for Alex while the other parent's claim is active.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child (Alex, age 4) who lives with them. While the child meets the age criteria and the claimant shares parental responsibility, Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for this child. Since only one person can claim Child Benefit for any given child at a time, the claimant is not eligible to make a separate claim.
